skill.name: hyperframes-media
skill.description: Preprocess media assets for HyperFrames compositions with local text-to-speech, transcription, and background removal workflows.
skill.summary: Create narration, transcripts, and transparent media overlays for HyperFrames projects.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, filesystem, and blocker patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples and media terminology. A real medium risk remains because the skill instructs agents to run local CLI commands that download models and process user files.

medium_findings.title: Local CLI Commands Download Models and Process Files
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run npx hyperframes commands for TTS, transcription, and background removal. This is legitimate for the skill, but it executes local tools, downloads model files, and reads or writes user media paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated. They appear purposeful for media processing rather than malicious, so the risk is operational rather than a confirmed compromise.

low_findings.title: Cache and Repository Paths Are Benign Filesystem Use
low_findings.description: The hidden home-directory findings refer to documented model cache paths under ~/.cache/hyperframes. The traversal alert is a repository-relative documentation link, not evidence of unsafe path handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are explanatory Markdown, cache locations, and a relative link. No code constructs or user-controlled path joins are present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The blocker findings match media terms such as model sizes, Whisper model names, IDs, CRF values, and HTML data attributes. No cryptographic API, hashing routine, system probing, or secret collection is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is media workflow documentation. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or reconnaissance behavior in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses shell command examples as its primary workflow. Marketplace users should understand that following the skill may execute local commands and create output files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit command invocations. The content supports legitimate media operations and does not show credential theft, obfuscation, or unauthorized network exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Video compositions often need narration, captions, and transparent overlays before design work can continue. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through local HyperFrames media preprocessing commands.
actual_capabilities: Generate local voiceover audio with Kokoro voices through the HyperFrames CLI.
Transcribe audio or video into word-level timestamp data for captions.
Import existing SRT, VTT, or OpenAI response transcripts into normalized output.
Remove image or video backgrounds to create transparent overlays.
Explain model, voice, language, and output format choices for media workflows.
limitations: Requires local CLI dependencies and model downloads before first use.
Does not provide an inpainting workflow for clean background plates.
Large Whisper and segmentation models can be slow and disk intensive.
Output quality depends on source audio, source video, and selected model settings.

use_cases.title: Create Narration for Product Videos
use_cases.description: Generate a voiceover file from a script and prepare it for a HyperFrames composition.

use_cases.title: Build Caption Timing from Existing Media
use_cases.description: Transcribe an audio or video file into word-level timestamps for animated captions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Presenter Cutouts
use_cases.description: Remove the background from presenter footage and export a transparent overlay.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Voiceover
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-media to create narration from script.txt with a warm professional voice. Save the output as narration.wav.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe a Video for Captions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-media to transcribe demo.mp4 for captions. Choose a safe Whisper model and preserve the original spoken language.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Transparent Presenter Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-media to remove the background from presenter.mp4. Export a transparent WebM suitable for a HyperFrames composition.

prompt_templates.title: Chain Narration and Captions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hyperframes-media to generate narration from script.txt, transcribe the result, and explain how to use the transcript for captions.

output_examples.input: A user asks for narration from a launch script.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a suitable Kokoro voice, runs the HyperFrames TTS command, and returns the created audio file path.

output_examples.input: A user provides Spanish product footage for captions.
output_examples.output: The assistant avoids English-only Whisper models, transcribes the media, and produces timestamped caption data.

output_examples.input: A user wants a presenter over animated graphics.
output_examples.output: The assistant exports a transparent presenter video and explains when to add a separate background plate.

best_practices: Confirm file paths, output names, and language before running local commands.
Use non-English Whisper models unless the user confirms English audio.
Test short media segments before processing large production files.
anti_patterns: Do not use English-only transcription models for unknown or non-English audio.
Do not treat a hole-cut background plate as an inpainted clean plate.
Do not run model downloads or media commands without user awareness.

faq.question: Does this skill require API keys?
faq.answer: The documented TTS workflow runs locally and does not require an API key.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: It guides HyperFrames CLI commands for Kokoro TTS, Whisper transcription, and u2net background removal.

faq.question: Can it remove a person and rebuild the empty room?
faq.answer: No. It can create transparent cutouts and hole-cut plates, but clean plates require inpainting.

faq.question: Can it handle non-English speech?
faq.answer: Yes, but the user should choose a non-English Whisper model or provide the language code.

faq.question: Does it download files?
faq.answer: Yes. The described commands download local model files on first use and cache them under the user cache directory.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it guides local command execution and file processing.
